extra_snippets: June 4, 2026  Researchers at EPFL have developed a chip-scale ultrafast laser that performs on par with traditional tabletop femtosecond lasers. The innovation could make advanced laser technologies far smaller, cheaper, and more accessible for applications ...

results.title: The Guardian view on how culture is taking on tech: the ultimate handheld device | Editorial | The Guardian
results.description: Above all, Transcription is about 21st-century anxiety, and particularly the experiences of children. Covid, environmental collapse, endless wars – technology is not the sole culprit. Thomas’s granddaughter will only eat once she is given an unlimited diet of junk food and YouTube videos.
extra_snippets: “The question of how certain forms of media flatten or monetise our attention – I do think that’s political territory,” the author said of his win. With its shiny black cover and stark white typeface, this slim novel is designed to remind to us that the book is also a handheld portable device. It invites us to consider the relationship between art and technology.
Editorial: Transcription, the winner of the Orwell prize for political fiction, and Toy Story 5 show life before smartphones and screen time

results.title: Council Post: The Importance Of The Connected Technology Stack For Small Businesses
results.description: While most small businesses have embraced digital tools, the real issue is that these tools often operate in isolation.
extra_snippets: Over the last decade, the technology market has become saturated with standalone applications designed to solve specific problems. One platform handles billing, another manages scheduling, a third tracks inventory and a fourth stores customer information.

Even the best software cannot perform effectively without reliable access to data and applications. Unstable connectivity leads to interrupted workflows, delayed transactions and poor customer experiences. Reliable LTE, 5G and managed Wi-Fi should be viewed as essential components of any technology strategy.

Technology investments should be evaluated based on business outcomes, not features. Before implementing new solutions, organizations should establish clear performance benchmarks and measure improvements over time.

By adopting connected ecosystems that integrate hardware, software, connectivity, security and support, small businesses can streamline operations, increase productivity and focus on what matters most. A connected technology stack is quickly becoming essential for sustainable growth.​​

results.title: As 'Terminator 2: Judgment Day' turns 35, it's time to accept the truth: Terminator shouldn't be back | Space
results.description: It's not just that the movies failed to change with the times, though… we've changed. The "Terminator" movies are a warning about the dangers of technology and AI. The problem with watching it now is that it doesn't feel like fiction anymore.
extra_snippets: The future has been written, and it's a dark fate for the Terminator franchise.

results.title: 7 Best Portable Tech Gadgets That Were Designed for the Job Site and Are Better for It Everywhere Else - Yanko Design
results.description: Most wearable safety technology carries its purpose visibly. It bulks up, goes tactical, and ends up being gear you wear only when you genuinely expect to need it. The O-Boy, developed by Brussels-based studio Futurewave, refuses that convention.
extra_snippets: What Futurewave got right, beyond the technology, is the design brief. The O-Boy does not read as survival gear. It looks like something a person who regularly spends time in remote environments would choose to wear rather than something they reach for when a trip turns serious.

The practical difference between this and a power station you remember to put in the bag is that the Handsfree 2 charges while you walk. Transit becomes charging time, which is a consequence of starting from job site requirements rather than outdoor recreation ones. Fragmented solar panel technology handles overcast days with reasonable efficiency.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?t=1&v=gdWt5OhoRe0 Most portable tech exists on a spectrum of compromise. It is either capable but too heavy, light but too fragile, or designed so carefully for the desk that the real world breaks it within a year. results.title: ROV Technology Leader Invests In Next-Generation Underwater Robotics Firm
results.description: Imenco Future Technologies, a global leader in remotely operated vehicle (ROV) technology, has announced it has taken a strategic investment stake in Frontier Robotics, a specialist developer of advanced subsea robotics technologies supporting its long-term innovation and growth strategy.
extra_snippets: The investment represents a significant step in Imenco Future Technologies’ ongoing commitment to advancing subsea capabilities, enhancing operational efficiency, and accelerating the development of next-generation solutions for offshore energy, defense, and emerging blue economy sectors.

The investment represents a significant step in Imenco’s Future Technologies ongoing commitment to advancing subsea capabilities, enhancing operational efficiency and accelerating the development of next-generation solutions for offshore energy, defense, and emerging blue economy sectors.

Jonatan Scharff Willners, Founder and CEO at Frontier Robotics said: “We are delighted to welcome Imenco Future Technologies as a strategic investor. Their deep domain expertise, global market presence, and commitment to innovation make them an ideal partner as we continue to grow and expand our technology portfolio.

Frontier Technologies will benefit from Imenco’s global reach, operational experience, and established customer base, providing a strong platform to scale its technologies and bring innovations to market more rapidly.

results.title: Advanced Technology Services Names Eric Martin President | citybiz
results.description: Advanced Technology Services (ATS) has appointed Eric Martin as president, expanding his leadership responsibilities as the industrial maintenance and reliability... Read More
extra_snippets: Advanced Technology Services (ATS) has appointed Eric Martin as president, expanding his leadership responsibilities as the industrial maintenance and reliability services provider aligns its sales, strategy and operations organizations to support continued growth.
Martin will oversee ATS’s sales, strategy and operations teams, with responsibility for advancing the company’s technology-driven maintenance and reliability solutions for manufacturers.
As president, Martin will focus on executing the company’s long-term growth strategy while expanding its portfolio of maintenance, reliability and technology-enabled industrial services.
Headquartered in Peoria, Illinois, Advanced Technology Services provides industrial maintenance, reliability, technology and parts services to more than 1,400 manufacturing facilities worldwide.

results.title: Belgian defense minister says Europe should tap Turkish technology - Türkiye Today
results.description: Belgian Defense Minister Theo Francken calls Türkiye ‘very important ally' that should be included in Europe’s defense programs
extra_snippets: Belgian Defense Minister Theo Francken said Türkiye is an important NATO ally and that Europe should make greater use of Turkish technology in defense production, ahead of the July 7-8 NATO meeting in Ankara.
He stressed the need for cooperation on defense investment, the NATO Force Model, increased production, air defense and new technologies.
Francken said the alliance’s second priority should be technology, air defense and increased industrial production, while the third should be continued support for Ukraine.
“We can learn a lot from your technology,” he added.

results.title: As Chinese Tech Pulls Ahead, U.S. Fears It Will Become Dependent - The New York Times
results.description: Chinese firms have some of the world’s most advanced technology. But U.S. officials say relying on it could come with a downside.
extra_snippets: In at least a few sectors, that equation has flipped, with China racing ahead of the United States. From batteries and solar panels to rare earths and life sciences, China is developing some of the world’s most advanced technologies, and it is rapidly deploying plans to corner other markets.

It has unveiled a battery that, according to the company, can power an electric vehicle for 250 miles with less than 10 minutes of charging — about three times the charging speed of batteries typically found in other electric vehicles. CATL’s battery technology is powering millions of cheap electric vehicles that China is exporting around the world.
The site, owned by Contemporary Amperex Technology Company Ltd., or CATL, is the world’s largest cluster of battery factories, and the most advanced.
For decades, American companies maintained a huge technological lead over their Chinese competitors. U.S. firms came to China to manufacture their goods more cheaply.

results.title: Tech giant Oracle cuts 21,000 jobs as it embraces AI
results.description: Oracle was co-founded by Larry Ellison in 1977. He remains its chief technology officer
extra_snippets: Oracle shed about 21,000 roles globally in the last year as the US technology giant reshapes its business around artificial intelligence (AI), the firm's latest annual report shows.
The "deployment of AI technologies across our operations have resulted, and may continue to result, in reductions to our workforce," the report says.
The company was co-founded by Larry Ellison, one of the richest people in the world, who also serves as Oracle's chief technology officer.
Google, Amazon and Meta collectively plan to pour some $650bn into the technology this year.

results.title: Vodafone Ireland and Government trial satellite technology to keep emergency services connected - Irish Tech News
results.description: Vodafone Ireland, in partnership with the Office of the Government Chief Information Officer (OGCIO), has successfully tested the integration of satellite
extra_snippets: Vodafone Ireland, in partnership with the Office of the Government Chief Information Officer (OGCIO), has successfully tested the integration of satellite technology for emergency and public service organisations.

Delivered under a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the OGCIO, the technology would keep emergency services running during severe weather events or major outages, when mobile networks can be impacted by disruption to their power supply.

Clare Island has played a central role in Vodafone Ireland’s satellite testing programme, including Ireland’s first mobile video call via satellite earlier this year. Its unique terrain makes it an ideal real-world environment for testing how satellite-enabled communications technologies can support rural communities and critical national infrastructure.

The trial showed how satellite connectivity can help maintain communications at all times, supporting access to 999 and 112 emergency services — regardless of mobile provider — as well as blue light organisations such as police, fire and ambulance services. Satellite technology provides an additional layer of connectivity alongside terrestrial networks, helping ensure that the public and emergency responders can stay connected when it matters most.
